text.title: CI Quality Gate Design Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that erode CI gate effectiveness. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Block-everything anti-pattern — total BLOCK runtime balloons past the budget.
failure-mode.detector: Sum of BLOCK runtimes > 1.5x budget target AND no plan to demote
failure-mode.repair: Apply r3 zero-sum rule. Demote the highest-runtime / lowest-actionable BLOCK to WARN or NIGHTLY. Document the demotion in the review log.

failure-mode.description: WARN-tier checks are not surfaced — they run but no one reads them.
failure-mode.detector: WARN check output is not posted as PR comment OR is collapsed behind 5+ clicks
failure-mode.repair: Add a PR-comment bot that surfaces WARN diffs inline. Or escalate the most-ignored WARN check to BLOCK (only if budget allows) or NIGHTLY (preferred). A silent WARN is dead weight.

failure-mode.description: No budget arithmetic — PRs creep to 20+ minute cycles without anyone noticing.
failure-mode.detector: ci-design.md lacks the budget block OR runtime numbers are > 90 days stale
failure-mode.repair: Populate the budget block from `scripts/pr-cycle-time.py` output. If actual is over target, trigger demotion or parallelization.

failure-mode.description: Owner = "the team" — unowned checks rot when they flap.
failure-mode.detector: owner column contains "team" / "everyone" / blank for >= 2 BLOCK checks
failure-mode.repair: Assign a named individual for each BLOCK check. Rotation OK (quarterly) but the field is never blank or collective. Update the escalation directory.

failure-mode.description: Skip-merge culture — `[skip ci]` or `--no-verify` used in > 5% of merges.
failure-mode.detector: Git log analysis shows skip markers in 5%+ of merge commits over last 60 days
failure-mode.repair: Investigate which check is being skipped most. Either fix the check (likely non-actionable, demote or improve) or remove the skip mechanism. Skip-merge is a symptom; the disease is a bad BLOCK check.

failure-mode.description: Config drift — ci-design.md says BLOCK but the workflow file allows pass-through.
failure-mode.detector: For each ci-design BLOCK row, the corresponding job in workflow lacks proper failure propagation (or has continue-on-error)
failure-mode.repair: Fix the workflow OR fix the document — pick the one that reflects intent. Add a lint script that diffs ci-design.md tier classifications against workflow configuration on every CI change.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: LLM helping with CI redesign must not autonomously add or remove BLOCK gates based on a code diff. All tier changes require human review and a review-log entry. AI is a drafter, not a gate-keeper.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly review is a human gate: the named owner of each BLOCK reviews their entry, confirms or updates rationale, and signs the review log. Reviews that lapse > 6 months auto-flag the check as candidate for WARN demotion.
